GS3.headline: Preliminary Findings of All India Tiger Estimation 2024 Show Promising Growth in Tiger Population
GS3.explainer:
• Preliminary results from the All India Tiger Estimation 2024 indicate a continued positive trend in India's tiger population.
• The census, conducted every four years, uses advanced methodologies including camera trapping and M-STrIPES software.
• While specific numbers are yet to be finalized, initial data suggests a healthy increase in several tiger reserves and new areas.
• This growth underscores India's commitment to tiger conservation and the success of various protection initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: All India Tiger Estimation is conducted every four years.
The 2024 census shows promising growth in tiger population.
Methods include camera trapping and M-STrIPES software.
India hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 for tiger conservation.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of India's tiger conservation efforts, particularly Project Tiger, in stemming the decline of tiger populations and contributing to global biodiversity.
Discuss the challenges faced in tiger conservation, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, and poaching, and suggest sustainable solutions.
Analyze the methodologies employed in the All India Tiger Estimation and their role in providing accurate data for conservation policy-making.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through a dense forest, symbolizing successful conservation.
GS3.trivia: India achieved its target of doubling the tiger population by 2022, four years ahead of schedule, set during the St. Petersburg Declaration in 2010.

GS3.headline: GST Collections for October Maintain Strong Growth Trajectory, Signal Economic Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections for October 2024 have shown robust growth, maintaining a strong upward trajectory.
• The consistent high collections indicate sustained economic activity and improved compliance across various sectors.
• This positive trend provides a healthy revenue stream for both central and state governments, supporting fiscal stability.
• Experts attribute the growth to festive demand, effective tax administration, and a formalization of the economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Fiscal policy
prelimsPoints: GST collections indicate economic health and compliance levels.
October 2024 collections show strong growth.
GST is a destination-based consumption tax.
It replaced multiple indirect taxes in India.
The GST Council is the governing body for GST.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India's fiscal architecture and its impact on economic growth and formalization of the economy.
Discuss the challenges faced in GST implementation and its evolution since introduction, including issues of compliance and revenue distribution.
Examine how sustained high GST collections contribute to government revenues and fiscal stability, particularly in the context of post-pandemic economic recovery.
GS3.imageDescription: A stack of Indian currency notes with the GST logo superimposed.
GS3.trivia: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017, through the 101st Constitutional Amendment Act.

GS3.headline: Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plan to Combat Rising Coastal Erosion
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Earth Sciences has finalized an Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) Plan to address the escalating problem of coastal erosion.
• The plan incorporates a multi-pronged strategy, including natural solutions like mangrove restoration, artificial reefs, and engineered structures.
• It emphasizes stakeholder participation, scientific data collection, and climate change adaptation measures for vulnerable coastal communities.
• Rising sea levels and extreme weather events have intensified coastal erosion, posing threats to livelihoods, infrastructure, and biodiversity.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) Plan is finalized.
It addresses coastal erosion, a major environmental concern.
Strategies include mangrove restoration and artificial reefs.
The plan emphasizes stakeholder participation and scientific data.
It is under the Ministry of Earth Sciences.
mainsPoints: Examine the causes and consequences of coastal erosion in India, highlighting its impact on coastal communities, biodiversity, and infrastructure.
Critically analyze the components of the Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) Plan and its effectiveness in mitigating coastal hazards and adapting to climate change.
Discuss the role of natural solutions versus engineered structures in coastal protection and the importance of community participation in sustainable coastal management.
GS3.imageDescription: A coastline showing signs of erosion, with a protective wall and newly planted mangroves.
GS3.trivia: About one-third of India's coastline is experiencing varying degrees of erosion.

GS3.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Expands Reach to Support Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is expanding its outreach, with a renewed focus on nurturing startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to early-stage startups for prototype development, market entry, and commercialization.
• This expansion aims to democratize the startup ecosystem, tap into local innovation, and create employment opportunities beyond metropolitan areas.
• The government expects this move to foster a more inclusive and geographically diversified startup landscape in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
GS3.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) supports early-stage startups.
The scheme is expanding its focus to Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
It provides financial assistance for prototype development and market entry.
The scheme is under the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT).
It aims to foster an inclusive and diversified startup landscape.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of government initiatives like the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme in fostering entrepreneurship and innovation in India.
Analyze the significance of expanding startup support to Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities in promoting inclusive growth and reducing regional disparities.
Examine the challenges faced by startups in non-metro cities and suggest policy measures to strengthen their ecosystem and access to funding.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic of a plant growing from a seed, symbolizing startup growth, with city skylines in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is home to the world's third-largest startup ecosystem, after the US and China.

sources: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
